In the report of the Spokane board
of health for the Year 1908, which is
just from the printer, the following
interesting comparison is given, which
shows Spokane's growth and develop
ment in one year. 1t is published in
the report for the benefit of the many
persons in the east and elsewhere who
are daily asking for copies of the
report. In each instance the first
figures given are for 1902 and the
second fer 1903. The comparisons are
as follows:

pills in public schools, 772l 1906; church
organizations, 81, 81; secret and benev-
olent societies, 100, 10; public library
volumes, 1886, 945s; public hospitals,
4, 7; number of passenger trains en-
tering and leaving the city on seven
railroads, 10, %; number of telephones
in use in the city, 1894, 5000.
